content.description:
Code that transforms an item into a visual DOM representation. Views are how items become visible and interactive.

## The Render Function

Every view exports a `render` function:

```javascript
export async function render(item, api) {
  // item: the item being rendered
  // api: the renderer API (storage, navigation, helpers)
  
  const div = api.createElement('div', { className: 'my-view' });
  div.textContent = item.content.title || 'Untitled';
  return div;  // Return a DOM node
}
```

## Required Fields

| Field | Purpose |
|-------|--------|
| `for_type` | UUID of the type this view renders |
| `code` | JavaScript with the `render` function |
| `displayName` | Human-readable name shown in view picker (optional) |

## View Discovery

When the system needs to render an item, it resolves the view through a preference hierarchy:

1. **Explicit**: View ID passed to `api.renderItem(itemId, viewId)`
2. **Contextual**: Parent's child spec has `view.type`
3. **Item preference**: Item's `preferredView` field
4. **Type preference**: Type definition's `preferredView` field
5. **Type chain walk**: Find view with matching `for_type`
6. **Fallback**: [system:default-view](item://aaaaaaaa-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) (JSON inspector)

## The Renderer API

The `api` parameter provides:

**Storage:**
- `api.get(id)` — Fetch an item
- `api.set(item)` — Save an item
- `api.query(filter)` — Search items

**Rendering:**
- `api.renderItem(id, viewId?)` — Render another item
- `api.rerenderItem(id)` — Re-render in place
- `api.createElement(tag, attrs, children)` — Create DOM elements

**Navigation:**
- `api.navigate(id)` — Navigate viewport to item
- `api.siblingContainer` — Add items as siblings in current container

**Context:**
- `api.IDS` — Well-known GUIDs
- `api.require(name)` — Load libraries
- `api.viewport` — Current viewport state

## Creating a View

```javascript
{
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  name: "my-note-view",
  type: "aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",  // kernel:view
  content: {
    for_type: "note-type-uuid",
    displayName: "Note View",
    description: "Renders notes with markdown formatting",
    code: `
      export async function render(item, api) {
        const md = await api.require('hobson-markdown');
        return md.render(item.content.description || '', api);
      }
    `
  }
}
```

## Multiple Views Per Type

A type can have multiple views for different purposes:
- **Compact**: Summary/card display
- **Full**: Complete detail view
- **Editable**: With editing controls
- **Readonly**: Display only

Users switch views via right-click → "View As..."

## View vs View-Spec

| Aspect | View (imperative) | [View-Spec](item://bbbbbbbb-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) (declarative) |
|--------|-------------------|------------------|
| Defined by | JavaScript code | JSON ui_hints |
| Flexibility | Full control | Constrained to field components |
| Complexity | Can be complex | Simple field layouts |
| Use when | Custom interaction | Standard form-like display |

## See Also

- [view-spec](item://bbbbbbbb-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Declarative view definitions
- [field-view](item://cccccccc-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — Field-level rendering components
- [kernel:rendering-system](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000) — View dispatch infrastructure
- [kernel:code](item://22222222-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) — How code items work
